Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Set For Order To Show Cause On May-24-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS KINK STUDIOS, LLC, CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC, ARMORY STUDIOS, LLC, PETER ACWORTH, HOGAN KARL AKA VAN DARKHOLME, JOSHUA CHATTERLEY AKA SEBASTIAN KEYS, AND CHRISTINE CORRIGAN AKA TOMCAT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, Defendants' demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Although plaintiff does not expressly allege that he was an independent contractor, nothing in the complaint or that is judicially noticeable establishes that he was an employee. The determination whether a person is an employee or independent contractor is done on a case by case basis involving the consideration of numerous factors. (S.G. Borello & Sons, Inc. v. State Dept. of Industrial Relations (1989) 48 Cal.3d. 341.) The Cal-OSHA Appeals Board Decision does not establish that plaintiff was an employee in this particular case. Nor does plaintiff's workers' compensation claim establish that plaintiff was an employee. The status of the workers' compensation proceeding is unclear and it is premature to determine whether judicial estoppel applies. Moreover, facts alleged in the complaint support exceptions to the exclusivity rule. (Labor Code 3601(a)(1) (injury caused by the willful and unprovoked physical act of aggression of another employee); 3602(b)(1) (willful physical assault) & (2) (fraudulent concealment aggravates injury). The alleged OSHA violations support plaintiff's negligence per se claim. (See Elsner v. Uveges (2004) 34 Cal.4th 915, 928.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
